rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. No earlier date available. The court's register of actions reflects objections just filed on 11/16/22. No courtesy copy of the objections has been received by the assigned examiner. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Courtesy copies of the objections and any other papers filed on or after 11/16/22 must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 1/26/23 to be considered. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN HENRY MCCOY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/23/2023, as the following issues e-mailed to counsel on 09/12/2022 remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE 120 form. 3) No inventory and appraisal has been filed. 4) Does the petitioner anticipate obtaining waivers of the first interim account from all other beneficiaries? The decedent died more than two years ago. The first interim account covering a two-year period from the decedent's date of death may be required absent the waivers. In addition, notice must be given to the person who filed a request for speical notice on 11/21/2022. Copies of the petition and all supplemental papers must also be served. An appearance may be required on 01/23/2023 if the issues are not fully addressed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS FOON LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] Papers should also be two-hole punched. No SASE was provided. A conformed copy of the order cannot be returned.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHOOI CHOOI LING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Even though a memorandum/note of tangible personal properties is attached to the will, the memorandum is not being offered as a part of the deceent's will, thus will not be admitted to probate as a part of the will. Status hearing on close estate is set for 12/11/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL LARRY MCLEAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. No earlier date available. PETITIONER IS STRONGLY URGED TO HIRE AN ATTORNEY. Only two complete courtesy copies have been received by the examiner as of 11/21/22: the defective proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate and the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License. Only the first pages of the petition, the Duties and Liabilities, the Request for Special Notice (which makes no sense, in any event), the premature and unnecessary Notice of Proposed Action, and something called Memorandum re death certificates to be "inserted" in the "probate folder" were sent to the examiner on 11/18/22 and received on 11/21/22. Still no proof of publication has been filed. No proof of service of 15 days' notice of this hearing has been filed. No verified supplement to the petition has been filed yet to identify the parents of the decedent and to set forth their dates of death or to correct item 6f of the petition. No waivers of bond have been filed. Examiners review filed papers; they may not provide legal or procedural advice. [LRSF 14.13.] Complete courtesy copies of ALL papers filed to date plus the verified supplement to the petition, proof of service of notice of the 2/6/23 hearing, and waivers of bond must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 1/26/23 to avoid another continuance. If not all heirs waive bond, bond will be required for the full estimated value of the estate. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2022. Verified supplemental declaration to be filed 7 days prior to povide further information - When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s)through whom they may take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s)to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. LRSF 14.18.C.(3). Also Propsoed Order must be submitted.
